questions.prompt: Default cache behavior of `fetch()` in a Server Component?

choices.text: Cached indefinitely (treated as static) until a rebuild or explicit revalidation. You opt out per call: `cache: 'no-store'` for fully dynamic, `next: { revalidate: 60 }` for time-based ISR, `next: { tags: ['posts'] }` for tag-based with `revalidateTag('posts')` to invalidate. The default-cache model means most data is fast and CDN-friendly; opt into dynamic only when content varies per request or per user. Dynamic functions (cookies(), headers(), searchParams) also force dynamic rendering for the page.

questions.explanation: Cached by default (static). Opt out via cache: 'no-store' or next.revalidate / next.tags.

questions.prompt: Dashboard fetches user, posts, and analytics — currently sequential, slow. Fix?

choices.text: Parallelize independent fetches with `Promise.all`: `const [user, posts, analytics] = await Promise.all([fetchUser(), fetchPosts(), fetchAnalytics()])`. Total time becomes max(each) instead of sum(all). Sequential await is only correct when fetch B depends on the result of fetch A — and even then prefer pushing to a single query (SQL JOIN beats two HTTP round-trips). Request deduplication is automatic: if two components both fetch the same URL during one request, the actual fetch happens once.

questions.explanation: Promise.all for independent fetches. Sequential is the trap.
